title: AMS resonance directory expanded with five Tier-1 cluster pages
content: canon/resonance/ expanded from one page (recursive-mas.md) to six pages with the addition of agent-messaging-protocol.md, a2a.md, mcp.md, acp.md, and nlip.md. Each new page covers one entrant in the 2025–2026 envelope-altitude cluster identified in ams://canon/principles/envelope-altitude-consensus. Each page follows the resonance template at klappy://canon/resonance/template (frontmatter, title with "(Resonance)", AMS Principle, Convergent Quote, Where AMS Aligns, Where AMS Diverges Explicit, Why the Divergence Matters, Operationalization in AMS, Related Canon). Tier 3 across the batch, matching recursive-mas.md. ams:// namespace per overlay convention.

title: The resonance batch is written fresh per entrant rather than reusing the in-thread AMP draft
content: The operator chose "fresh passes" at the planning gate rather than promoting the in-thread AMP comparison from earlier in the session. Each page in the batch was written from a fresh read of the source repo or specification rather than from session-internal summary. The fresh-pass discipline produces sharper entrant-specific divergences (e.g. AMP's trust annotations, A2A's Agent Cards, MCP's tool schemas, ACP's lifecycle-as-OTLP-spans, NLIP's CBOR mandatory binding) that the in-thread summary did not surface. Confirms the value of the canon rule that resonance pages are mechanical comparisons grounded in the source, not session-internal essays.

title: MCP gets a resonance page despite being structurally cooperative with AMS
content: MCP is the protocol AMS uses (D0006 makes the MCP wrapper the canonical edge-wrapper class). The resonance page exists not because MCP is an alternative to position against, but because the resonance convention requires explicit divergence statements for any cited work. Without a resonance page, the AMS-uses-MCP relationship has no addressable canon surface — only the long-form §11.4 note. The page promotes the §11.4 framing into addressable canon and lets sibling pages (especially acp.md, since ACP extends MCP) cross-reference cleanly. Future cooperative-not-competitive protocols that AMS depends on may follow the same pattern: a resonance page documents the layer relationship and the explicit altitude divergence, even when the relationship is composition rather than competition.

title: The shared-principle-first sequencing made the batch genuinely cheaper
content: With ams://canon/principles/envelope-altitude-consensus already encoded as the shared root, each per-entrant page in the batch was 8–10KB and focused on entrant-specific consequences rather than re-deriving the wire-altitude argument. By contrast, recursive-mas.md (written before any shared principle existed) is 9.5KB of which ~30% restates the load-bearing argument internally. The DRY-the-meta-first sequencing the operator chose produced both shorter pages and sharper per-entrant divergences. Recommendation for future cluster surveys: encode the shared root as canon principle first whenever the divergences across candidates have a common shape.

title: canon/resonance/ now has six entries and still no README index
content: Per the standing observation from 2026-05-02-ams-recursive-mas-resonance.tsv ("MD indexes are a lie in wait"), canon/resonance/ continues without an index file. With six entries the temptation to add one grows. Counter-pressure: the index would have to be hand-maintained, and the same discoverability is achievable via oddkit_search and via the cross-reference graph in each page's "Related Canon" section. The pages already cross-link to siblings (each new page references at least one other resonance page in Related Canon). Open question retained: whether the no-README-index stance generalizes across all canon/ subdirectories or remains a per-directory choice.

title: Tier-2 candidates next: ROS, Matrix/Mastodon, ANP
content: Next planned scope returns to planning, then execution: the Tier-2 resonance candidates from the survey screening matrix. ROS (Robot Operating System) is the structural ancestor — topics + publishers + subscribers near-identical to streams + writer + subscribers — with sharp divergences (master node/registry vs registry-less, intra-fleet vs cross-org, typed messages vs opaque tokens). Matrix/Mastodon are already informally positioned in AMS.md §11.3 ("we considered adapting them") and would promote to canon/resonance/matrix.md and canon/resonance/mastodon.md (or a single combined page) by extracting the existing positioning. ANP (Agent Network Protocol) needs a fresh read-and-screen pass before promotion — the survey identified it from the arxiv:2505.02279 paper but the deeper read has not happened. Tier-3 candidates (LangGraph/AutoGen/CrewAI as a single class-page on centralized-orchestration frameworks) and the standing security-as-subscriber-pattern principle are the next-after-next candidates.

title: Two future canon principles named but not yet planned
content: Two candidate Tier-2 principles surfaced in the conversation following the resonance batch: (1) "security as subscriber pattern" — the analog of D0010 for security; signing, audit, policy enforcement, and anomaly detection live as subscribers that join conversations the same way operators and observers do; intersects with the per-conversation runtime isolation journal entry that already named the threat model gap; (2) "the inversion principle, generalized" — promoting "doing less enables more" from the AMS-local per-query-dynamic-orchestration framing into a klappy.dev upstream principle covering substrate-design across multiple layers (TCP/IP, Unix pipes, HTTP, AMS as repeated instances of the same bet). Neither is urgent. Both are real. Re-entry signal for #1 is a real security incident or a new entrant making a security claim that needs answering. Re-entry signal for #2 is the next time the inversion is the load-bearing argument in a piece of canon being authored.

title: canon/resonance/{agent-messaging-protocol,a2a,mcp,acp,nlip}.md committed
content: Five files committed to canon/resonance/. Total 45,638 bytes across the batch. Each page: full frontmatter (uri ams://canon/resonance/<slug>, title, audience=canon, exposure=nav, tier=3, voice=neutral, stability=stable, tags, epoch=E0008.4, date=2026-05-02, derives_from including ams://canon/principles/envelope-altitude-consensus, governs, status=active); 7 H2 sections matching recursive-mas.md (AMS Principle, Convergent Quote, Where AMS Aligns, Where AMS Diverges Explicit, Why the Divergence Matters, Operationalization in AMS, Related Canon); each cites ams://canon/principles/envelope-altitude-consensus 3x (frontmatter derives_from, body of Where AMS Diverges, Related Canon); each adds at least one entrant-specific divergence beyond the shared root per the constraint encoded in 2026-05-02-ams-envelope-altitude-consensus.tsv. Voice-cliché audited against klappy://canon/constraints/ai-voice-cliches: zero formulaic transitions, zero summary clichés, zero sycophantic openers, zero puffing-importance hits across all five files; em-dash density flags in mcp.md, acp.md, nlip.md were verified as false positives (all three are "Where AMS Diverges" bullet lists matching recursive-mas.md's exact formatting pattern, where each em-dash separates a bolded headline from its explanation as distinct semantic work). Canon graph: each new page links to at least one sibling page in Related Canon, producing a cross-reference graph instead of an index file (per the standing "MD indexes are a lie in wait" observation).
